title: Geography of Global Conflict

jokes.text: Oh, boy, spoiler alert. Somebody just won a ride in the wheelchair.
jokes.setup: Student gives perfect answer about WWII and Yalta Conference
jokes.punchline: Teacher sarcastically threatens to make her disabled like FDR for being too eager
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts the typical teacher-student dynamic with unexpected cruelty. Well-structured with good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Wow. Okay, I'm going to have to break your legs and put you in a wheelchair just to make you cool.

jokes.text: Highlight? Probably the backpack.
jokes.setup: Britta mentions getting a see-through yellow pen to 'color in the words'
jokes.punchline: When asked if she means highlight, she thinks it's asking what her favorite thing is
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Britta's cluelessness about basic academic terms while trying to be serious about school.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I got this see-through yellow pen so I can color in the words.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You mean highlight?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What? No, highlight is my favorite thing.

jokes.text: My first cop flashlight. Can't wait to get some brains on this bad boy.
jokes.setup: Chang excited about security guard equipment
jokes.punchline: Eagerly anticipates violence with disturbing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang violent enthusiasm. Great character integration - only Chang would say this with such glee.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Finally, some real equipment! A badge, a baton, a radio... Oh man, I've been waiting for this moment my whole life.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You know what this means? I can finally use force. Legally. On people. Beautiful.

jokes.text: The badge says to serve and protect! No, it doesn't. 'How's my smile?' Oh, man.
jokes.setup: Chang confronting someone while referencing his badge
jokes.punchline: His badge apparently says something about smiling instead of police mottos
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag subverting expectations about what a security badge would say. Good misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You see this badge? It says 'Have a nice day.' I'm not a real cop.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We know, Chang.

jokes.text: Please? I swear they're just for sex!
jokes.setup: Chang begging to keep some equipment
jokes.punchline: His justification makes it worse by mentioning sexual use
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes his plea worse. Extremely economical and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Please, I need this equipment. I use it for... educational purposes. And recreational purposes. And... intimate purposes.

jokes.text: How progressive of you to have a multicultural evil twin. An Asian Annie. Obama's America.
jokes.setup: Meeting Annie Kim who seems similar to Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff frames it as diversity progress while calling her evil
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about TV tropes and racial representation, typical Jeff cynicism with cultural commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, this is great. We're really making progress on diversity.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We have two Annies now. Annie and... evil Annie.

jokes.text: You be careful, Annie. They are ruthless. What? What? Not Asians. Women.
jokes.setup: Pierce warning Annie about competition
jokes.punchline: Clarifies his prejudice is about gender, not race
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce misdirection revealing unexpected prejudice. Perfect character consistency and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Annie, you're going to face some serious competition out there. I'm not going to lie to you.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: From who?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: From women.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's... that's sexist, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm not sexist. I'm not saying women are worse than men. I'm just saying they're different. And that difference is that they're women.

jokes.text: You mean the younger you that took so much Adderall she tried to straighten the lines on our high school football field during a game?
jokes.setup: Annie comparing Annie Kim to a younger version of herself
jokes.punchline: Jeff recalls Annie's extreme perfectionist drug-fueled behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific absurd detail. Great character integration referencing Annie's backstory.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, I remember freshman year Annie. She was like you, but angrier, and on several medications.

jokes.text: Too bad the fascist oligarchies are raping them to make hamburgers. Look it up.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to be activist about rainforests
jokes.punchline: Oversimplified conspiracy theory ending with 'look it up'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta pseudo-activism with the classic 'look it up' ending that shows her shallow understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: The rainforests are disappearing because of corporate greed and government corruption. They're cutting down trees to make room for cattle ranches owned by people who don't even care about the environment. It's all connected to pharmaceutical companies who want to suppress natural remedies because they can't patent trees. Wake up, sheeple! Look it up.

jokes.text: Looks like someone woke up on the regular side of the bed.
jokes.setup: Response to Britta's activist rant
jokes.punchline: Subverts 'wrong side of the bed' by calling normalcy 'regular side'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - Troy's innocence making Britta's activism seem abnormal.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Britta, did you wake up on the wrong side of the bed?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: There is no wrong side of the bed, Troy. That's just a saying designed to make people feel bad about their natural sleep patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So you woke up on the regular side?

jokes.text: Maybe my path is a war path that leads to the terrordome, n-words!
jokes.setup: Britta's building activist rant about her life path
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly tries to use offensive language but self-censors
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Britta moment - trying to be edgy but immediately backing down. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I've spent my whole life trying to find my purpose, you know? I've been a cop, a psychologist, a therapist... and every time I think I'm on the right path, something goes wrong. But that's not going to stop me anymore. I'm taking control of my destiny, and anyone who stands in my way can just... can just go f— uh... frick themselves.

jokes.text: Don't worry. She'll be bad at it.
jokes.setup: Concerns about Britta's dangerous-sounding activism
jokes.punchline: Jeff dismissively predicts Britta will fail at rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff cynicism and economy. Captures both characters perfectly in minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta's going to get herself killed doing whatever dangerous activist thing she's doing now. She'll probably mess it up though. Britta fails at everything she tries to do that's remotely rebellious or important.

jokes.text: Keep clear of the can. Sir, step around the can. Mind the can, student.
jokes.setup: Chang's new security duties
jokes.punchline: His entire job is just warning people about a garbage can
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pattern showing the absurd mundanity of Chang's new role. Sets up running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: His entire job is just warning people about a garbage can

jokes.text: I piss warnings, pig.
jokes.setup: Chang giving Britta a warning
jokes.punchline: Britta's attempt at tough rebel talk comes out awkward
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta trying to be tough but sounding ridiculous. Very economical and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You better watch yourself, Perry. These streets are dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well maybe I like danger. Maybe I'm into it. Maybe I'm a rebel who does... rebel things... dangerously.

jokes.text: And meet Kofi Annan, Boutros Boutros Ghali, and Will.I.Am. Boutros Boutros Ghali?
jokes.setup: Annie listing prestigious UN figures she might meet
jokes.punchline: Includes random celebrity Will.I.Am in list of diplomats, Jeff questions the oddest name
jokes.explanation: Absurd inclusion of pop star with diplomats, Jeff focuses on wrong odd name

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I could meet Ban Ki-moon, or Kofi Annan, or Will.I.Am.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Will.I.Am is not a UN diplomat.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, not with that attitude.

jokes.text: They used to call me model U.N. guy back in college. Well, that's not-- don't research that.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming expertise in model U.N.
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracks suggesting his nickname wasn't for the reason he implied
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where character realizes his claim sounds suspicious. Nice structure with immediate backtrack.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: I have extensive experience with Model U.N. In fact, they used to call me 'The Diplomat.'

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: Though, to be fair, that was mostly because I kept accidentally sitting on the chairs.

jokes.text: Cligoris? Either pronunciation is fine?
jokes.setup: Learning the professor's name
jokes.punchline: Name sounds like female anatomy and professor is defensive about pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Basic name-sounds-like-body-part humor. Fairly predictable and not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So what's the professor's name?

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: It's Pubis. Professor Pubis.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Like... pubis?

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: It's pronounced 'PEW-bis,' actually.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Sure, that's definitely going to catch on.

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: My family name has been mispronounced for generations, and frankly, I'm tired of the smirking.

jokes.text: So I've had your photo around my neck since I was in high school.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming to be Clark M. Eichelburger (U.N. founder)
jokes.punchline: Implies obsessive devotion to obscure historical figure
jokes.explanation: Weird specific detail that creates creepy character moment. Good originality in the oddly specific devotion.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: I am Clark M. Eichelburger, founder of the United Nations.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: I've dedicated my entire life to studying and honoring his legacy. Every waking moment is consumed by his achievements, his vision, his... his very existence.

jokes.text: and the diabolical little b--usybody stabbed her in the back.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing Annie Kim's actions
jokes.punchline: Starts to say 'bitch' but catches himself with 'busybody'
jokes.explanation: Standard self-censoring gag. Functional but not particularly creative or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: She's such a b— busybody.

jokes.text: I didn't know that all you had to do was call dibs.
jokes.setup: Conflict over who started the model U.N.
jokes.punchline: Reduces intellectual property to playground rules
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that diminishes the opponent's claim with childish framing. Good economy and bite.

dialogue.speaker: Annie Kim
dialogue.line: I started Model U.N. at Greendale!

dialogue.speaker: Annie Edison
dialogue.line: No, I did. I've been running it for years.

dialogue.speaker: Annie Kim
dialogue.line: Well, I'm the real Annie Kim, so it's mine.

dialogue.speaker: Annie Edison
dialogue.line: That's not how intellectual property works.

dialogue.speaker: Annie Kim
dialogue.line: I called it first. Finders keepers.

jokes.text: Is he your father or your lover?
jokes.setup: Jeff and Annie's protective dynamic
jokes.punchline: Professor bluntly asks about inappropriate relationship
jokes.explanation: Awkward direct question that makes everyone uncomfortable. Economical but fairly obvious observation.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: So Jeff, Annie... you two aren't... you know?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? No! She's like... she's like a daughter to me.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: He's like a father figure.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: That's not better.

jokes.text: We're friends. Yeah. Good friends. Grown-up friends. Yeah. So. We're friendly.
jokes.setup: Question about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward attempts to define their friendship
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of discomfort as they try to explain their relationship. Well-structured awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We're just friends.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Best friends?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Friends who happen to... spend a lot of time together.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Friends with history?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Friends with... complicated history.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So like, best friends?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why do you keep saying that?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Because you're being weird about it!

jokes.text: Somalia has 1,900 miles of coastline, a government that knows its place, and all the guns and wives you can afford to buy.
jokes.setup: Pierce reading about Somalia for model U.N.
jokes.punchline: Focuses on problematic aspects with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - finding all the wrong things appealing about a country. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Did you know Somalia has no central government? It's a failed state! And the piracy! The piracy is incredible!

jokes.text: Why have I never heard of this paradise before?
jokes.setup: Pierce's description of Somalia
jokes.punchline: Calls war-torn Somalia a 'paradise'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that reinforces Pierce's skewed worldview. Economical and character-consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Somalia is a paradise.

jokes.text: Troy, Georgia the country, not the state. That's right. Capital city Tbilisi, and former member of the Soviet Union. And we kindly request y'all mind your Ps and Qs.
jokes.setup: Troy learning about the country Georgia
jokes.punchline: Uses Southern accent despite representing the European country
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - Troy does his research but still uses wrong accent. Perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, Georgia? I thought we were talking about the country!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well bless your heart, y'all. Georgia is a fine state with pecan pie and sweet tea.

jokes.text: Does two U.N.s mean there are two earths? Uh, yeah. Sure, two earths.
jokes.setup: Model U.N. competition setup
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately finds logical problem, professor dismissively agrees
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed meta-analysis that the professor can't handle. Excellent character integration and originality.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: This Model U.N. scenario has a logical inconsistency. The resolution references Article 51 of the UN Charter, but the fictional country we're representing didn't exist until 1993, making the reference anachronistic.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: Yes, well... good catch, I suppose.

jokes.text: This is what the United Nations is doing to your freedom. I'm your freedom! And I'm in a cage. With the world! Because of them!
jokes.setup: Britta protesting the model U.N.
jokes.punchline: Incoherent metaphor where she represents freedom in a cage
jokes.explanation: Peak Britta protest logic - passionate but completely nonsensical. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You can't cage freedom! I'm like a... a bald eagle wrapped in the American flag, but the flag is also a cage, and the cage is the American flag, and I'm the freedom that's being caged by the very symbol of freedom!

jokes.text: He's pulling my cage. I'm being pulled in my cage for no reason! Is anybody seeing this? Is the world seeing this? Are we facebooking this?
jokes.setup: Britta being dragged away by Chang in her protest cage
jokes.punchline: Appeals for documentation of her 'oppression' with modern social media terms
jokes.explanation: Britta wanting to document her failed protest, mixing serious activism with social media

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! Is anyone live-tweeting this? Someone Snapchat it! Post it on Instagram!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is oppression! People need to see this! Hashtag free Britta!

jokes.text: Uruguay sounds like 'you're a gay.' Uruguay kindly requests that Somalia stop pronouncing it 'you're a gay.'
jokes.setup: Fast-paced model U.N. session
jokes.punchline: Pierce keeps mispronouncing Uruguay as homophobic slur
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay but works for Pierce's character. The formal diplomatic response makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Uruguay's delegate is recognized.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: The delegation from [homophobic slur that sounds like Uruguay] moves to—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's Uruguay, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's what I said, [repeats slur].

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No, you're saying it wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: [Says slur again], right?

jokes.text: Oh! Damn, Pierce. Ooh, that wasn't me. I don't smell anything-- whup, I just got it. Oh, it's in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Pierce apparently farted during model U.N.
jokes.punchline: Everyone's reactions escalate from denial to disgust
jokes.explanation: Basic fart humor with escalating reactions. Low-hanging fruit but the escalation adds some structure.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Did you just...?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No. No way.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That happened.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Lord have mercy!

jokes.text: You're as much a criminal as this idiot is a cop. None whatsoever.
jokes.setup: Chang bringing in Britta for 'making a mess'
jokes.punchline: Dean completely dismisses Chang's authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect put-down with great economy. Dean's deadpan delivery makes Chang's authority meaningless.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Dean, I'm bringing Britta in for making a mess.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Chang, I don't have time for this. Britta, you're free to go.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: But Dean, I'm the security guard. I have authority here.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: No, you don't.

jokes.text: You know the thing that comes down, keeps the...cars from coming in. The thing. It's like a gate, but it's just a stick. Comes down, from a hinge. Keeps the cars out. Goes up and down 90 degrees. The arm?
jokes.setup: Chang trying to report something broken
jokes.punchline: Elaborate description of simple parking arm because he doesn't know the word
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating description of something simple. Chang's inability to find the word creates great tension.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Dean, Dean! We need to talk about the parking lot. There's this... this metal thing that goes up and down, you know? It's red and white striped, and it blocks cars from going through, and when you push a button or something it just... rises up into the air like it's surrendering.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Chang, that's a parking arm.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I know what it is, I'm just describing it to you!

jokes.text: So whoever actually died a few months ago, 'fess up so we can put a stake through your heart.
jokes.setup: Group trying to figure out source of terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Jeff suggests one of them is actually dead and decomposing
jokes.explanation: Jeff's dark humor explanation for the smell, mixing vampire and zombie mythology

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, I have a theory. What if one of us is actually dead and we just don't know it yet? Like, what if the smell is just... us decomposing?

jokes.text: Noooooo! I think the lesson here is that I need to win this! I want to win! And you! You suck! You're a stupid, ugly poopface! I'm better than you!
jokes.setup: Annie's competitive breakdown
jokes.punchline: Devolves into childish insults despite trying to be mature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character breakdown showing Annie's competitive nature. Great escalation from mature to childish.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to be the bigger person here. I'm mature enough to rise above this petty bickering.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You're a big stupid head with a stupid face and stupid hair!

jokes.text: You're acting like a little schoolgirl and not in a hot way.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to calm Annie down
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate qualifier reveals his thoughts
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff inappropriate comment. Very economical and reveals character's mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, Annie, calm down. You're being a little too... intense.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Too intense? Jeff, you literally told me I needed to be more assertive!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I said assertive, not... hot.

jokes.text: If embarrassment were bountiful zinc deposits, I'd be Zambia.
jokes.setup: Annie embarrassed by her outburst
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor using model U.N. knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant original metaphor that shows Annie's intelligence while expressing embarrassment. Highly quotable and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to yell. It's just... I feel like I'm a small nation that's been ignored by the Security Council, and everyone's passing resolutions about me without even letting me speak!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, that's... actually a pretty specific metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I've been preparing for Model U.N. since I was seven!

jokes.text: There's nothing to be Zambia about, Annie.
jokes.setup: Responding to Annie's Zambia metaphor
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Zambia' as verb, playing off her metaphor
jokes.explanation: Jeff playing along with Annie's elaborate metaphor by treating country name as verb

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is... I Zambia'd the whole thing?

jokes.text: when you feel the way I feel about you, the easy loophole through the creepiness and danger is to treat them like a child. 'A chip off the old block, you're the best, kiddo.'
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his relationship with Annie
jokes.punchline: Admits to using condescension to avoid addressing attraction
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on Jeff's behavior pattern. Great character insight and self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I use condescension as a defense mechanism. It's easier to talk down to Annie than to actually acknowledge that I'm attracted to her.

jokes.text: Can't keep doing this forever, kiddo. Can't we? Nope. I can't, no. Nope, that's gross. That feels gross.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to break the 'kiddo' pattern
jokes.punchline: Immediate discomfort when they try being closer
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of discomfort showing why Jeff uses the 'kiddo' crutch. Great character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to stop calling you 'kiddo.' We're going to be... closer.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah. From now on, I'm calling you... Annie.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's my name.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This isn't working.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Nope.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Kiddo?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Much better.

jokes.text: would it help if I said I farted? Annie, no. I can't let you fall on that sword because... I farted. No, I farted. I farted.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to take blame for the smell
jokes.punchline: Everyone claims responsibility in Spartacus-style
jokes.explanation: Good Spartacus parody with fart humor. The callback structure elevates basic toilet humor.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It was me. I did it. I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, it was me.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I'm Spartacus!

jokes.text: Guys, I wasn't doing the Spartacus thing. I farted.
jokes.setup: Group doing Spartacus fart confession
jokes.punchline: Troy actually did fart and isn't being noble
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection subverting the noble gesture. Very economical and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I am Spartacus!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, I actually did it. I really farted.

jokes.text: You can't just mumble nonsense. No one's cutting away. Okay, fine, here's my actual plan.
jokes.setup: Abed apparently mumbling his plan
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaks fourth wall acknowledging TV editing conventions
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about TV conventions. Perfect for Community's style and Abed's character.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: So I'll just stand here and mumble my whole plan, and then later they'll show a flashback of me explaining it clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not how real life works.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No. We're not on a TV show.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Then how do you explain the camera?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What camera?

jokes.text: We represent the United Nations from Earth I. Using our Switzerland's hadron collider and resources from every nation, the people of our planet have constructed a trans-dimensional gateway here.
jokes.setup: Model U.N. competition finale
jokes.punchline: Abed creates elaborate sci-fi solution using earlier Earth II concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that brings back the Earth II concept. Perfect Abed solution that's both logical and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: What if we propose a joint Earth II initiative? We establish dual planetary governance systems, each country maintains sovereignty while sharing technological advancement protocols.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: That's... actually brilliant. The judges are eating it up.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Furthermore, we create a secondary Earth II as a backup, with a third Earth II as insurance for the backup, and a fourth Earth II exclusively for storing backups of the other Earth IIs.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, now you're just saying Earth II.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Exactly. I've solved world peace through recursive planetary duplication. It's foolproof.

jokes.text: One which flies in the very face of the United Nations itself, a fundamentally symbolic organization founded on the principles of high-minded rhetoric and empty gestures.
jokes.setup: Annie Kim's practical rejection of symbolic gesture
jokes.punchline: Professor declares symbolic gestures are what the U.N. is actually about
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about the U.N.'s actual nature. Academic humor that's both cynical and accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Cligoris
dialogue.line: Symbolic gestures are what the U.N. is actually about.

jokes.text: I told you to stop playing Operation on me! I'm not your damn board game!
jokes.setup: Chang apparently being operated on
jokes.punchline: Compares medical procedure to children's board game
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reference that fits Chang's dramatic personality. Solid but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: What are you doing to me? This feels like Operation!

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: It is Operation.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Well, why didn't you say so? I'm great at that game!

jokes.text: At least you didn't get my spare ribs.
jokes.setup: Chang complaining about being operated on
jokes.punchline: Pun on spare ribs (body part vs food)
jokes.explanation: Basic pun that doesn't really land. Fairly predictable wordplay with minimal creativity.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: They took out my spare ribs!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Your spare ribs?

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Yeah, apparently I have more ribs than a normal person. The doctor said I'm like a side of beef.

tmdb_overview: As Annie competes with an overachieving rival in a Model United Nations competition, Jeff wrestles with his feelings for his ambitious friend.
insight: Escalation-driven cringe comedy carries a mid-tier episode through 46 jokes in 46 minutes.
